'''Jilst Bindalin''' was a [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adept]] [[Homeworld/Legends|hailing from]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]]. At some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], he learned the location of a [[Killik Sith warrior's tomb]] on the world [[Sarafur]] and, after stealing an artifact, traveled there. Bindalin then stored the artifact in the [[Unidentified Killik Sith warrior|Sith's]] [[Burial/Legends|burial]] as a form of vengeance and was subsequently never seen again.

==Biography==
Jilst Bindalin was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] individual who lived<ref name="Tessent" /> at some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="3 ABY">By default, the events of {{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}} are set during the opposition to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars (1977) 69|''Star Wars'' (1977) 69]], which depict the [[Battle of the City of Bone|end]] of the Imperial presence on Mandalore per {{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}, to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> He was a [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adept]] and a prodigal son of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]]. At some point, Bindalin learned of an old [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanen]] legend about the flight of an ancient [[Unidentified Killik Sith warrior|Killik Sith warrior]] from Alsakan to the<ref name="Tessent" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> world [[Sarafur]], where the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] had [[Death/Legends|died]] and been [[Burial/Legends|buried]].<ref name="Tessent" />

The Alsakani subsequently located the [[Killik Sith warrior's tomb|Killik's tomb]] and stole an artifact. He later hired a Shistavanen [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] to transport him and the artifact, which the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] adept kept in an [[Iron/Legends|iron]] case that he did not let out of his grasp, from the Core Worlds to the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]], where Sarafur was located. During the flight, Bindalin did not divulge any details regarding his goals in the Moddell. The Shistavanen captain left the Alsakani on Sarafur, after which the dark side adept was not seen in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] again. Bindalin then entered the Killik Sith's tomb and stored the artifact there as a form of petty vengeance.<ref name="Tessent" />

{{RPG|"[[Race for the Tessent]]"}}
At some point by 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], a group of [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunters]] arrived on Sarafur during a search for the artifacts known as the [[Tessent]]s. The group explored the Killik Sith warrior's tomb, discovering a vaguely [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Skeleton/Legends|skeleton]], as well as a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], in the [[Water/Legends|waters]] present in the structure.<ref name="Tessent" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Race_for_the_Tessent_G9.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Jilst Bindalin was introduced in the roleplaying adventure "Race for the Tessent."]]
Jilst Bindalin was introduced in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Race for the Tessent]]," which was published for use with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Tessent" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref>

Instead of featuring a set plot, "Race for the Tessent" presents several settings in the Moddell [[Sector/Legends|sector]], Sarafur being one of them, that the player characters may visit during their search for the Tessent artifacts. For each of the settings, the adventure also suggests possible circumstances for a Tessent being hidden there, with the section dedicated to Sarafur noting how at some point Bindalin could have stolen the [[Alsakan Tessent]] and brought it to the Killik Sith warrior's tomb on the planet. Assuming that as much of the non-contradictory information presented in the adventure as possible is [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, this article assumes that the Alsakan Tessent was instead hidden on the planet [[Ast Kikorie/Legends|Ast Kikorie]].<ref name="Tessent" />
